data.title: Gainers & Losers: 10 stocks that moved the most on May 29
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Sensex, Nifty break 2-day loss, wraps third straight monthly series in green
data.description:
Markets recover on expiry day; metal stocks lift Nifty

Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty rebounded from intraday lows to close higher in a choppy trading session on May 29, driven by volatility linked to the monthly F&O expiry. Despite the swings, gains were registered across most sectors, with the exception of FMCG and PSU banks, which ended in the red. Meanwhile, India VIX, a key volatility gauge, dropped sharply by 8 percent, signaling a temporary cool-off in market nervousness.

The Sensex was up 320.70 points or 0.39 percent at 81,633.02, and the Nifty was up 81.15 points or 0.33 percent at 24,833.60. About 1948 shares advanced, 1877 shares declined, and 126 shares unchanged.

Broader markets also displayed strength, as both midcap and smallcap indices advanced by up to 0.5 percent, underlining investor confidence. A part of the positive sentiment was attributed to global cues, especially after a US federal court blocked former President Trump’s blanket tariffs on imports from US trading partners—boosting risk appetite worldwide.

Among the sectoral indices, the Nifty Metal index emerged as the top gainer, following Jefferies’ bullish stance on Indian steelmakers. The global brokerage highlighted the role of recently imposed safeguard duties in aiding steel profitability. It issued a buy rating on Jindal Stainless, while reiterating Tata Steel and Coal India as favorable investment picks.

As the May derivatives series concluded, the Nifty index posted approximately 2 percent gains, marking its third consecutive monthly advance.

Looking ahead, Sameet Chavan, Head of Research, Technical and Derivative at Angel One, noted that the psychological resistance for Nifty now lies at 25,000, with immediate support around 24,600, which coincides with the 20-day exponential moving average (20DEMA).

“While the benchmark indices continue to trade within a defined range, the action has shifted to specific themes and stock-level opportunities. Traders should focus on these pockets but remain cautious, as some recent breakouts have lacked follow-up buying,” Chavan cautioned.

Adding to the outlook, Arbind Maheshwari, Managing Director and Head of Equities at BofA Securities, echoed a more tempered view.

“Nifty’s valuations, at 21 times forward earnings for 10 percent earnings per share (EPS) growth, are fairly priced. While the long-term structural growth drivers remain intact, the market is likely to consolidate around current levels through the rest of the year,” he said in an interview with CNBC-TV18.

data.title: Waaree Energies shares gain 7% on its US arm securing order worth $176 million
data.description:
Waaree Energies shares gain up to 7% on its arm securing order worth $176 million.

Waaree Energies share price surged as much as 7 percent on Thursday after its wholly-owned subsidiary bagged a mega order worth $176 million.

Shares of the company surged to a high of Rs 2,994.5 per share on the NSE, rising 7 percent. The stock has been gaining for the last two days.

The company in an exchange filing, said "Waaree Solar Americas, a wholly owned subsidiary of Waaree Energies Limited has received an order on May 28, 2025 for supply of 586 MW solar modules for an amount of 176 Mn USD from a renowned customer who is a developer and owneroperator of utility scale solar and energy storage projects across the United States."

The supply of modules is scheduled to commence from the financial year 2026-27, the company said in an exchange filing.

data.title: Leela Hotels shares to be allotted to all retail bidders as IPO allotment expected today; check GMP and other details
data.description:
Leela Hotels shares to be allotted to all retail bidders.

All retail investors who applied for shares in the Rs 3,500-crore initial public offering (IPO) of Schloss Bangalore Pvt Ltd, which operates Leela Palaces, Hotels and Resorts, are likely to receive full allotment as the retail portion of the issue remained undersubscribed.

The share allotment is expected to be finalised today, May 29. Applicants can check their allotment status on the website of KFin Technologies Ltd, the registrar to the issue, using either their PAN or application number. The status will also be available on the BSE and NSE websites.

Leela Hotels IPO: Step-by-step guide to check allotment status on registrar, BSE and NSE

The IPO saw an overall subscription of 4.50 times, but demand from retail individual investors (RIIs) fell short, with the category subscribed only 83 percent. This means all retail bidders are set to receive the full quantity of shares applied for.

This subdued retail response, seen also in recent IPOs, marks a change in investor sentiment. The investors are reportedly weighing factors such as valuations, business fundamentals, and promoter track record before placing bids. With a crowded IPO pipeline expected in the coming months, many are choosing to be more selective.

Check All IPO News

This is the first time since 2022 that average retail participation in IPOs has shown a decline over a calendar year, signalling a broader shift in strategy among individual investors.

Retail investors turn selective with fresh issues as Leela, Aegis IPOs fail to evoke euphoria

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The IPO of Schloss Bangalore comprised a fresh issue as well as an offer for sale by existing shareholders. The proceeds are expected to be used for debt repayment and general corporate purposes.

Shares of the company will be listed on the exchanges on June 2.

data.title: Samvardhana Motherson Q4 results: Net profit jumps 20% to Rs 1,050 crore, firm announces 1:2 bonus issue
data.description:
Samvardhana Motherson Q4 results: Net profit jumps 20% to Rs 1,050 crore, firm announces bonus issue

Samvardhana Motherson on May 29 reported a net profit of Rs 1,050.5 crore for the fourth quarter of the financial year 2025. This marks a nearly 20 percent jump from the Rs 878.63 crore net profit reported in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year.

The company’s revenue from operations meanwhile rose around 6 percent on-year to Rs 29,316.83 crore in Q4 FY25. It had earlier reported a revenue of Rs 27,665.92 crore for Q4 FY24.

Along with the Q4 results, the company announced that its board has recommended a bonus issue of 1 shares for every 2 shares held by a shareholder. “These bonus shares are subject to approval of shareholders in the ensuing General Meeting,” the company said.

Samvardhana Motherson also announced a final dividend of Rs 0.35 per equity share for the financial year 2025. The record date to determine the eligibility of the shareholders set to receive the payment has been set on June 23. If approved by the shareholders at the company’s AGM, the dividend will be paid on or before September 26.

Our performance demonstrates the resilience and adaptability of our business. Leveraging our strong engineering and manufacturing expertise, we are well-equipped to meet our customers' needs, positioning us for long-term sustainable growth. Our booked business value of USD 88+ billion, which also encompasses non-automotive businesses, sets a strong foundation for a bright future. The Board congratulated the Motherson Team for an exceptional performance over the five-year period despite numerous challenges in a volatile world. The Company managed to achieve the highest ever sales outpacing the automotive industry by building several new facilities, integrating record number of acquisitions (23), entering new industries successfully, all while exceeding customer expectations on quality (receiving multiple awards globally); yet retaining focus on free cash flow and bringing the leverage ratio to the lowest level in the five year period," said Vivek Chaand Sehgal, Chairman of the company.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Auto firms may hit a steel wall in FY26 as 12% safeguard duty looms
data.description:
While some relief could emerge in the second half of the fiscal year—either through cost-cutting measures or improved pricing power—the first half is expected to be tough.

India's decision to impose a 12 percent safeguard duty on certain steel imports is set to raise the heat on auto manufacturers, with the sharpest sting expected for commercial vehicle (CV) makers like Ashok Leyland, Tata Motors and Mahindra and Mahindra. As steel forms a significant part of vehicle input costs, especially in trucks and buses, the additional levy could well inflate costs and eat into margins just as demand begins to cool off, analysts say.

CVs, which use the most steel among all vehicle categories, stand at the epicenter of this impact. According to Ajit Mishra, Senior Vice President at Religare Broking, “Steel forms 60 percent of commodity input costs for CVs.” With the new duty kicking in, Shridhar Kallani, Auto Analyst at Axis Securities, expects a 60 to 100 basis points margin hit for both PV and CV manufacturers in the first quarter of FY26, while two-wheeler makers could see a smaller impact due to lower steel content per unit.

Also read: 'We’ll bury you in orders': Defence secretary pitches for R&D investment by private sector

Ashok Leyland has already flagged concerns on profitability. In its post-Q4 earnings call, CEO and MD Shenu Agarwal acknowledged that steel costs are rising and would pressure margins in the short term. However, he struck a more optimistic tone for the longer run, saying the safeguard duty—valid for only 200 days—could have a limited impact, and that the company’s margin levers, such as premiumisation, cost control, and after-sales services remain intact.

Still, others in the segment may not have as much cushion. Tata Motors and Mahindra & Mahindra, which also operate in the steel-heavy CV space, are likely to feel the squeeze as well. “The entire CV segment will face profitability headwinds in the near term,” Ajit Mishra, Senior Vice President at Religare Broking, told Moneycontrol.

Passing on the added cost to consumers could be a tall order. With demand in both CV and PV segments already showing signs of moderation, OEMs may hesitate to hike prices. “OEMs might attempt to pass on higher steel costs to consumers in Q1, but feasibility depends on demand elasticity and competition. In the current demand environment, passing on costs might be challenging,” Shridhar Kallani said.

Read more: Cooling inflation gives confidence of durable alignment over 12-month horizon: RBI annual report

If companies do push through price hikes, they risk losing buyers in an already fragile demand environment. This could trigger another round of discounting, particularly in the PV and CV segments, leading to a further hit on profitability. As Shridhar Kallani put it: “Higher costs could pressure volumes or lead to discounting, especially in segments already facing a slowdown.”

The immediate impact will begin surfacing in Q1 FY26 earnings. However, the pain could deepen in Q2 as OEMs deplete lower-cost steel inventory and start procuring at elevated rates. While some relief could emerge in the second half of the fiscal year—either through cost-cutting measures or improved pricing power—the first half is expected to be tough.

data.title: Castrol India shares rise 6% on report of fresh buying interest for BP's lubricant biz
data.description:
Castrol India shares rise 6% on report of fresh buying interest for BP's lubricant biz

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Castrol India Ltd increased 6% on May 29 after Bloomberg reported that BP Plc’s Castrol lubricant business is attracting interest from energy companies including Reliance Industries Ltd. and buyout firms such as Apollo Global Management Inc. and Lone Star Funds.

The report added that a deal could fetch between $8 billion and $10 billion.

At 1:45 pm on May 29, Castrol India shares were trading 6% higher at Rs 219 apiece. The 52-week high of the stock is Rs 284.4 and 52-week low is Rs 162.6 apiece. The market capitalisation of the stock is Rs 21,600 crore.

BP has sent out initial information to other potential bidders for the unit, including Brookfield Asset Management and Stonepeak Partners, the report added.

The bidders would join Saudi Aramco in considering bids for all or part of the business.

As part of a major corporate revamp, BP started a strategic review of the Castrol-branded business. Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has emerged as one of the company’s largest shareholders and has been pressing management to make bolder changes.

In February, BP said it is looking at strategic review of its global lubricants business.

"Castrol is a leading and trusted global lubricants brand, marketing premium products in more than 150 countries and serving customers and consumers in the automotive, marine, industrial and energy sectors. Castrol has significant growth ambitions, including growing its core mobility businesses, expanding participation in industrial lubricants, enhancing its mobility services and diversifying into data centre fluids," said BP.

data.title: Avanti Feeds shares surge 8% on strong profit, margins in Q4FY25
data.description:
Avanti Feeds jumps 8 percent after posting nearly 40 percent profit growth in Q4FY25

Shares of Avanti Feeds surged 8 percent to Rs 882 apiece on May 29 following a strong financial performance in the March quarter (Q4FY25).

So far this year, the stock of this shrimp feed player has surged 32 percent, as against 4 percent rise in the benchmark Nifty 50 index.

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

The company reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 157 crore for Q4FY25, marking a 39.6 percent year-on-year (Y-o-Y) increase from Rs 112.6 crore in the corresponding quarter of the previous fiscal year (Q4FY24). The robust bottom-line growth was attributed to improved profitability and steady revenue expansion.

Revenue from operations stood at Rs 1,385 crore in Q4FY25, reflecting a 7.9 percent Y-o-Y increase from Rs 1,284 crore in Q4FY24.

Operational efficiency played a key role in the improved performance. Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) rose 36.4 percent Y-o-Y to Rs 176.7 crore, compared to Rs 129.5 crore a year ago. Additionally, EBITDA margin improved to 12.76 percent from 10.09 percent, signalling stronger cost management and better realisations.

The company’s board of directors has recommended a final dividend of Rs 9 per equity share of face value Rs 1 for the financial year 2025 (FY25), subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming Annual General Meeting (AGM).

Avanti Feeds operates in the aquaculture industry, focusing on the production of premium shrimp feed, running a Vannamei shrimp hatchery, and processing and exporting shrimp to global markets.

data.title: Insecticides India shares jump 8% as profit surges 85% YoY in Q4
data.description:
Insecticides India jumps 8 percent after robust Q4 showing

Shares of Insecticides (India) climbed 8 percent to Rs 895 per share on May 29 after the agrochemical company reported a robust performance for the March quarter (Q4FY25).

Over the past month, the stock has surged 23 percent, sharply outperforming the Nifty 50 index, which rose only 2 percent during the same period.

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

The company’s consolidated profit after tax (PAT) increased 85 percent year-on-year (Y-o-Y) to Rs 13.89 crore in Q4FY25, up from Rs 7.52 crore in the same quarter of the previous financial year (Q4FY24).

Revenue from operations also posted a strong showing, rising 32 percent Y-o-Y to Rs 358.92 crore in Q4FY25, compared to Rs 272.50 crore in Q4FY24.

At the operational level, earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) soared 226 percent Y-o-Y to Rs 28.47 crore, up from Rs 8.73 crore a year earlier. Correspondingly, EBITDA margin expanded by 470 basis points, reaching 7.9 percent in Q4FY25 versus 3.2 percent in Q4FY24.

Adding to its momentum, Insecticides India launched 11 new products in FY25, including offerings based on patented latest-generation technologies.

In a move to strengthen its backend and supply resilience, the company also acquired Kaeros Research Pvt. Ltd. This acquisition is expected to improve long-term sourcing capabilities, reduce costs via direct imports, and secure critical supply chains.

Additionally, Insecticides India successfully completed a buyback of 500,000 fully paid-up equity shares at Rs 1,000 per share, amounting to Rs 50 crore in total.

Commenting on the results, Managing Director Rajesh Kumar Aggarwal stated, “We are pleased to report a robust performance of the company, marked by strong execution and strategic discipline, resulting in a profit growth of 39 percent in FY25. This performance underscores the success of the strategic framework we established — centered around profitable growth, a sharper focus on premium products, and margin enhancement.”
